Weather Today [27 May, 2026]: Check IMD Rain Predictions, Heat Alert, Monsoon & Thunderstorms Across Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu, Hyderabad & More — Full City-Wise Forecast - The Sunday Guardian,https://news.google.?oc=5,The Sunday Guardian,2026-05-27T12:15:58+05:30,en,Karnataka,,"""heatwave"" Karnataka","Weather Today, 27 May 2026: India is witnessing one of the sharpest weather contrasts of the season as extreme heat continues to grip northern and central states while thunderstorms and heavy rainfall impact southern, eastern and Himalayan regions. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), several states remain under severe heatwave warnings with temperatures crossing 45°C in parts of Delhi, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh and Madhya Pradesh while at the same time, Kerala, Karnataka, Telangana and northeastern states are experiencing widespread rain and gusty winds.

Meteorologists say the weather pattern is being shaped by strong dry continental winds in the north and moisture-laden systems advancing from the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal while the delayed progress of the southwest monsoon has further intensified heat buildup across inland India.
Will it Rain or Snow in the Hills?
Western Himalayan regions including Jammu and Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand are likely to witness:
- Fresh snowfall possible in upper reaches of Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh
Why Is The Heat Building Up So Fast Across India?
- Delayed monsoon onset has prolonged dry weather conditions
- Persistent western dry winds are pushing temperatures higher
- Urban heat island effect is intensifying temperatures in metro cities
- Low overnight cooling is causing dangerous warm nights
- Heatwave conditions are strongest across northwest and central India
- IMD predicts relief may begin after May 29 with an incoming western disturbance
Where are the Heat Wave Alerts?
- Red Alert: Vidarbha, Eastern Madhya Pradesh
- Orange Alert: Delhi, Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, Western Madhya Pradesh
- Severe Heat Conditions: Uttar Pradesh and interior Maharashtra
- Temperature Range: 44°C to 47°C in several northern cities
- Warm Night Conditions: Delhi, Jaipur, Lucknow and surrounding regions
Is Monsoon Arrived
- Southwest monsoon has not officially reached Kerala yet
- Earlier forecast predicted onset around May 26
- Updated arrival window is now expected between June 2 and June 4
- Monsoon advancement remains active over Bay of Bengal and Arabian Sea
- Nearly 70% of India’s annual rainfall depends on southwest monsoon activity

FAQ’s
1. Are There Any Travel Or Safety Warnings?
Yes. Heatwave alerts remain active across northern India, while thunderstorm warnings are in place for southern and eastern states.
2. Will Temperatures Rise Across The Country?
Temperatures are expected to remain high in north and central India for the next two days before gradual relief begins.
3. Will There Be Any Rain Today?
Yes. Rainfall and thunderstorms are likely in southern India, eastern states and Himalayan regions.
4. Where Will It Snow Today?
Light snowfall is possible in higher reaches of Jammu and Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh.
5. Has The Monsoon Officially Reached Kerala?
No. The monsoon onset has been delayed and is now expected between June 2 and June 4.",1.0
